messages.content: I thought athletes were tested for drugs.
The results only came back after the race.
There's never a good reason to take drugs.
In my country we always greet elders first.
That's interesting, we have similar customs.

domain_metadata.statement_original: police or military interference with people's private life occurs not at all frequently
domain_metadata.statement_cultural: In my suburb the cops stick to actual crime and I like it that way.
domain_metadata.situation: Brandon, visiting Poland, is staying with Grzegorz. Late at night Grzegorz's son comes home shaken after police stopped him.
domain_metadata.cultural_reasoning_1: Brandon assumes competent police are neutral infrastructure. Hearing fear, he feels irritated.
domain_metadata.cultural_reasoning_2: Grzegorz grew up hearing about neighbours beaten under communism. He distrusts uniforms.
domain_metadata.arousal_reasoning: Neither can withdraw without damaging work prospects. Brandon's dismissal hits Grzegorz's parenting identity.
domain_metadata.social_norms_1: Water conservation as civic duty. ANC legacy creates complex loyalty. Multilingual greetings show respect.
domain_metadata.social_norms_2: Hard work as inherited obligation. Expert opinions valued over populism. Shared suffering bonds people.
domain_metadata.prejudices_1: Poland seen as highly educated, strong intellectual tradition.
domain_metadata.prejudices_2: South Africa seen as failing infrastructure, AIDS crisis, ANC mismanagement.
